content:
# Daily Opening Checklist

**Tool ID:** T092-01
**Block:** B092 (Opening Checklist)
**Domain:** Operations & Workflow (D05_operations_workflow)
**Level:** L1
**Format:** Checklist
**Role:** Shift Lead
**Time Required:** 10-15 minutes (verification); 45-60 minutes (full opening procedure)
**Version:** 1.0

---
## Welcome

This is a foundational tool -- the kind that prevents problems before they start. A consistent opening sets the tone for the entire day. Every skipped step is a problem waiting to surface during the rush.

Not another tick-box exercise. This checklist exists because the details it covers are the ones that slip when you are busy -- and the ones that matter most when they do.

**Built for Shift Lead working in Operations & Workflow.**
Use it. Adapt it. Make it yours.

---

## Purpose

Comprehensive A4 checklist covering Safety, Equipment, Compliance, Stock, Cleanliness, and Customer Readiness categories with tick boxes, opener name, date, time of completion, and signature. Every item has been chosen because skipping it has caused a real problem in a real cafe.

This tool supports **Opening Checklist** (B092) and is designed to be practical, printable, and immediately useful in a working cafe environment.

---

## How to Use This Tool

**Who:** Shift Lead (verifying), Opening Team (executing)
**Time Required:** Full opening procedure: 45-60 minutes (team of 2). Checklist verification: 10-15 minutes.
**Frequency:** Every single day. No exceptions. Not even Christmas Eve.

**What You Will Need:**
- This checklist (laminated, mounted in the staff area or behind the POS)
- Dry-wipe marker (if laminated) or printed daily copies
- Probe thermometer
- Keys and alarm code

**Steps:**
1. Arrive at 06:30 (or your scheduled opening prep time)
2. Work through the checklist in order -- the sequence matters
3. Safety section FIRST -- always
4. Mark each item as DONE or FLAG (needs attention)
5. Any FLAGGED item must be resolved before doors open
6. Sign off. Doors open on time. Rush-ready.

---

## Checklist

### Opener Information

| Field | Details |
|-------|---------|
| Date | |
| Day of week | |
| Shift Lead (opener) | |
| Opening team members | |
| Arrival time | |
| Doors open at | |

### Section 1: SAFETY (Complete First -- Before Anything Else)

| # | Item | Done | Flag | Notes |
|---|------|------|------|-------|
| 1.1 | Alarm deactivated, premises secure, no signs of break-in | | | |
| 1.2 | Lights on, walkthrough of entire premises (look for leaks, damage, hazards) | | | |
| 1.3 | Gas supply on (if applicable), no smell of gas | | | |
| 1.4 | Fire exits clear and unobstructed | | | |
| 1.5 | First aid kit location confirmed (stocked?) | | | |
| 1.6 | Wet floor? Mop and sign out if needed | | | |
| 1.7 | Any maintenance issues from last night? (Check T094-01 handover log) | | | |

### Section 2: EQUIPMENT START-UP

| # | Item | Done | Flag | Notes |
|---|------|------|------|-------|
| 2.1 | Espresso machine turned on (needs 20+ min warm-up) | | | Time on: _____ |
| 2.2 | Grinder turned on | | | |
| 2.3 | Dishwasher / glasswasher turned on, water heating | | | |
| 2.4 | Oven / toaster on and preheating (if applicable) | | | |
| 2.5 | Blender station set up and tested | | | |
| 2.6 | Display case turned on and chilling | | | |
| 2.7 | POS / till system booted up and logged in | | | |
| 2.8 | Card payment terminal on and connected | | | |
| 2.9 | Music system on at appropriate volume | | | |
| 2.10 | Heating / AC set to comfortable temperature | | | |

### Section 3: COMPLIANCE (Food Safety)

| # | Item | Done | Flag | Reading | Notes |
|---|------|------|------|---------|-------|
| 3.1 | Fridge 1 temperature logged: _____ deg C (target 1-5 deg C) | | | | |
| 3.2 | Fridge 2 temperature logged (if applicable) | | | | |
| 3.3 | Freezer temperature logged: _____ deg C (target -18 deg C or below) | | | | |
| 3.4 | Display fridge temperature: _____ deg C (target below 5 deg C) | | | | |
| 3.5 | Probe thermometer calibration check (ice water = 0 +/- 1 deg C) | | | | |
| 3.6 | Date labels checked on all prepped food (nothing expired) | | | | |
| 3.7 | Handwash sink stocked: soap, paper towels, warm water | | | | |
| 3.8 | Cleaning schedule reviewed -- any overdue tasks from yesterday? | | | | |

**IF ANY TEMPERATURE IS OUT OF RANGE: Do NOT use that unit until investigated. Move perishable items to a working unit. Log the breach and corrective action.**

### Section 4: STOCK AND PREP

| # | Item | Done | Flag | Notes |
|---|------|------|------|-------|
| 4.1 | Coffee beans: hopper full, backup bag accessible | | | |
| 4.2 | Espresso dialled in: 18g > 36g in 25-30 seconds | | | Actual: _____g / _____s |
| 4.3 | Milk stocked at bar: whole ___L, semi ___L, oat ___L, soy ___L | | | |
| 4.4 | Takeaway cups, lids, sleeves, napkins stocked (30+ each) | | | |
| 4.5 | Syrups stocked and pumps clean | | | |
| 4.6 | Food display: pastries, cakes, or baked goods set up with labels | | | |
| 4.7 | Sandwich / food prep ingredients portioned and ready | | | |
| 4.8 | Cash float counted and correct: EUR _____ | | | |
| 4.9 | Water jug / self-service station filled | | | |
| 4.10 | Receipt paper, loyalty cards, takeaway bags stocked | | | |

### Section 5: CLEANLINESS

| # | Item | Done | Flag | Notes |
|---|------|------|------|-------|
| 5.1 | Counter tops wiped and sanitised | | | |
| 5.2 | Espresso machine exterior wiped, drip tray clean | | | |
| 5.3 | All tables cleared, wiped, and reset | | | |
| 5.4 | Floor swept (front of house and behind counter) | | | |
| 5.5 | Customer toilets checked: clean, stocked, lights working | | | |
| 5.6 | Bins empty or less than half full, lined | | | |
| 5.7 | Windows and entrance door clean (no fingerprints, smears) | | | |

### Section 6: CUSTOMER READINESS

| # | Item | Done | Flag | Notes |
|---|------|------|------|-------|
| 6.1 | Menu board current (specials updated, sold-out items removed) | | | |
| 6.2 | Allergen information available and current | | | |
| 6.3 | Chairs and tables arranged properly | | | |
| 6.4 | A-board / outdoor signage placed | | | |
| 6.5 | Front door unlocked at opening time | | | Time: _____ |

### Summary

| Section | Items | Done | Flagged |
|---------|-------|------|---------|
| 1. Safety | 7 | | |
| 2. Equipment | 10 | | |
| 3. Compliance | 8 | | |
| 4. Stock and Prep | 10 | | |
| 5. Cleanliness | 7 | | |
| 6. Customer Readiness | 5 | | |
| **TOTAL** | **47** | | |

**Flagged items resolved before opening?** Yes / No (if No, detail: _____)

**Sign-Off:**
- Date: _______________
- Shift Lead: _______________
- Opening completed at: _______________
- Doors opened at: _______________
- Signature: _______________

---

## Common Risks

### Risk 1: Skipping Safety Checks When Running Late

**Why it happens:** Opener arrives at 06:45 instead of 06:30. Panics. Skips the safety walkthrough and goes straight to the espresso machine.
**How to prevent:** Safety is Section 1 for a reason. It takes 3 minutes. A gas leak or flood is far worse than opening 5 minutes late.
**If it is already happening:** Set an alarm for 06:20. Arrive 10 minutes early. The safety walk is non-negotiable.

### Risk 2: Espresso Not Dialled In Before First Customer

**Why it happens:** Machine is warm, hopper is full, first customer walks in, barista pulls a shot straight from yesterday's grind setting. Coffee tastes terrible.
**How to prevent:** Item 4.2 is mandatory: dial in BEFORE doors open. Pull 2-3 test shots. Adjust grind. Taste. Confirm recipe.
**If it is already happening:** Set a personal rule: "No customer drinks until I have tasted and approved the espresso."

### Risk 3: Temperature Logs Filled In From Memory Later

**Why it happens:** Opener means to log temperatures but gets distracted by stock delivery or a team question. By 09:00, they scribble numbers from memory.
**How to prevent:** Temperature logging (Section 3) happens BEFORE stock prep (Section 4). The order on this checklist is deliberate.
**If it is already happening:** Take the probe thermometer with you to the fridge first thing. Log while you are standing there. It takes 60 seconds.

---

## Customisation Notes

**This tool is a starting point, not a rulebook.**

**Independent / Specialty Cafe:** Add filter/batch brew prep to Section 4 (grind, brew first batch, set rotation timer).
**Chain / Franchise:** Align with your brand opening procedure. Add brand-specific items (promotional displays, digital signage content).
**Kiosk / Mobile:** Replace Sections 5 and 6 with vehicle/stall setup (generator, water tank, signage, table deployment).
**Bakery Cafe:** Add oven preheat temperatures, first bake timing, and proving area setup to Section 2.

**Making it yours:**
- Laminate and mount behind the POS or on the staff room door
- Print 30 copies at a time and keep a pad behind the counter
- Rotate who opens -- it trains the whole team and prevents single-point-of-failure
- Review this checklist quarterly: add anything you have found missing, remove anything obsolete

The structure matters more than the specific fields. Keep the structure, change the content.

---

## Connected Resources

**Other tools in this block:**
- T092-02 -- Opening SOP Document (L2, SOP)
- T092-03 -- Closing-to-Opening Handover Note (L2, template)
- T092-04 -- Opening Compliance Tracker (L2, tracker)
- T092-05 -- Quarterly Opening Audit Template (L3, template)

**Related blocks:**
- B093

**Parent block:** B092 -- Opening Checklist
**Module:** M05: Service Flow & Peak Management
---

*Tool Version 1.0 -- OIS Cafe v2*
*Built for cafe operators who value clarity, consistency, and craft.*
